emails.subject: Risk Magazine Questions
emails.body: risk magazine has requested an interview with enron regarding risks=20 associated with the western power markets. eric thode has lined me up to= =20 talk with them. after seeing the questions that they are interested in, it= =20 seems to me that they are most interested in understanding legal and=20 contractual risks in the west. while i am happy to talk with them, you guy= s=20 are the experts on these matters. what do you think of the questions posed= =20 below? should someone from the legal team talk to them instead of me? let= =20 me know your thoughts. Forwarded by Tim Belden/HOU/ECT on 04/18/2001 08:13 AM=20 Eric Thode@ENRON 04/17/2001 02:41 PM

emails.subject: Risk Magazine Questions
emails.body: risk magazine has requested an interview with enron regarding risks=20 associated with the western power markets. eric thode has lined me up to= =20 talk with them. after seeing the questions that they are interested in, it= =20 seems to me that they are most interested in understanding legal and=20 contractual risks in the west. while i am happy to talk with them, you guy= s=20 are the experts on these matters. what do you think of the questions posed= =20 below? should someone from the legal team talk to them instead of me? let= =20 me know your thoughts. Forwarded by Tim Belden/HOU/ECT on 04/18/2001 08:13 AM=20 Eric Thode@ENRON 04/17/2001 02:41 PM

emails.subject: Re: Risk Magazine Questions
emails.body: Re: Risk Magazine Questions The questions are primarily legal/credit related. I will make sure Elizabet= h=20 and Mark are aware of this and that we discuss it as soon as we can. I=20 personally prefer NOT to talk to any reporter and if a lawyer is going to d= o=20 the job, it should be Elizabeth of Mark. My imagination's tendency to=20 "contemplate" could kill us in print. ----cgy
